blocks:
Sūrat al-Nāzi‘āt

Makkan, and it is 46 verses.

Sūrat al-Nāzi‘āt

Makkan. The number of its verses is forty-six according to the Kūfī count, and forty-five according to the rest. Two Verses *وَلِأَنْعامِكُمْ ** (And for your cattle) is the reading of the Ḥijāzī and Kūfī schools.* طَغى ** (He transgressed) is the reading of the ‘Irāqī and Shāmī schools.**

بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) وَالنَّازِعاتِ غَرْقاً (1) By those who tear out violently, (٢) وَالنَّاشِطاتِ نَشْطاً. (2) by those who draw out gently, (٣) وَالسَّابِحاتِ سَبْحاً (3) by those who glide swiftly, (٤) فَالسَّابِقاتِ سَبْقاً (4) by those who race ahead, (٥)** فَالْمُدَبِّراتِ أَمْراً ** (5) by those who regulate the affairs!

These are descriptions (صفات) of the Angels of Death. Allah swore by them concerning the establishment of the Hour (قيام الساعة). The object of the oath (جواب القسم) was omitted because what follows indicates it. They are those who violently tear out the souls (أرواح) of the disbelievers (الكفّار) from their bodies (أبدانهم) with severity. غَرْقاً ** (violently) means plunging deeply (إغراقاً) into the extraction (النّزع), just as the one who draws the bowstring (النازع في القوس) plunges deeply, reaching the limit of the draw (غاية المدّ). And they draw out (ينشطون) their souls (أرواحهم), meaning they extract them from between the skin and the nails until they bring them out from their insides (أجوافهم) with distress (الكرب) and grief (الغمّ). And they seize the souls (أرواح) of the believers (المؤمنين), drawing them out gently (سلّا رفيقاً), then they leave them until they find rest, like one swimming with something in the water and casting it off. Then they race ahead (تسبق) with the souls of the believers to Paradise (الجنّة). And the angels regulate (تدبّر) the affairs (أمر) of the servants (العباد) from year to year.

This is likewise mentioned in al-Majma‘, narrated from ‘Alī (peace be upon him) and from al-Ṣādiq (peace be upon him): It is death (الموت); the souls (النفوس) are extracted.

And al-Qummī narrated from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him) regarding: فَالسَّابِقاتِ سَبْقاً ** (by those who race ahead), meaning the souls (أرواح) of the believers; their souls race ahead to Paradise (الجنّة).

(٦) يَوْمَ تَرْجُفُ الرَّاجِفَةُ ** (6) The day the quaker quakes, Al-Qummi said: The earth will split open with its inhabitants. (٧) تَتْبَعُهَا الرَّادِفَةُ ** (7) followed by the subsequent one. He said: al-Rādifah (الرادفة) is the Cry (الصيحة). (٨) قُلُوبٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ واجِفَةٌ ** (8) Hearts on that day will be throbbing, Severely agitated due to the throbbing (الوَجِيف). (٩) أَبْصارُها خاشِعَةٌ ** (9) their eyes downcast. Meaning, the eyes of their owners will be humbled (ذليلة) out of fear, and for this reason, He attributed them to the hearts (القلوب). (١٠) يَقُولُونَ أَإِنَّا لَمَرْدُودُونَ فِي الْحافِرَةِ ** (10) They say, 'Shall we indeed be restored to the former state? [It means] to the first state (الحالة الأولى), meaning life after death. [This is derived] from their saying: "So-and-so returned to his ḥāfirah (حافرته)," meaning the path (طريقته) by which he came, and he "dug" (حفرها) it, meaning he left a trace (أثر) in it by his walking. Al-Qummi said: Quraysh said: "Shall we return after death?"

(١١) أَإِذا كُنَّا ** (11) 'When we are' And it was read (قُرِئَ) as إذا كنّا (when we are) as a statement (على الخبر). عِظاماً نَخِرَةً ** decayed (بالية). And it was read (قُرِئَ) as نخرة (نَخِرَة) and it is more emphatic (أبلغ). (١٢) قالُوا تِلْكَ إِذاً كَرَّةٌ خاسِرَةٌ ** (12) They say, 'In that case, it will be a losing return!' possessing loss (ذات خسران). And al-Qummi (said): If it is true, then we are losers because of our denial of it, and this is mockery (استهزاء) from them. Al-Qummi said: They said this in the manner of mockery (الاستهزاء). (١٣) فَإِنَّما هِيَ زَجْرَةٌ واحِدَةٌ ** (13) Yet it will be only a single shout, meaning, do not find it difficult, for it is nothing but a single cry (صيحة واحدة), meaning the second blast (النفخة الثانية). (١٤) فَإِذا هُمْ بِالسَّاهِرَةِ ** (14) and behold, they will be on the surface of the earth. and behold, they are alive upon the surface of the earth, after they were dead inside its belly. And بِالسَّاهِرَةِ ** is the white, level earth (الأرض البيضاء المستوية). Al-Qummi said: The shout (الزجرة) is the second blast (النفخة الثانية) in the Trumpet (الصور), and al-Sāhirah (الساهرة) is a place in Syria (بالشّام) near Jerusalem (بيت المقدس). And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), regarding His saying أَإِنَّا لَمَرْدُودُونَ فِي الْحافِرَةِ : He says: [It means] into the new creation (الخلق الجديد). As for His saying فَإِذا هُمْ بِالسَّاهِرَةِ : al-Sāhirah (الساهرة) is the earth. They were in the graves (القبور), and when they heard the shout (الزجرة), they came out of their graves and stood level upon the earth.

** هَلْ أَتاكَ حَدِيثُ مُوسى ** (15) Has the story of Moses come to you? Has not his story already come to you, so that it may console you regarding the denial of your people and their threats against you, by showing that they will be afflicted by the like of what afflicted those who were mightier than them? ** إِذْ ناداهُ رَبُّهُ بِالْوادِ الْمُقَدَّسِ طُوىً ** (16) When his Lord called him in the sacred valley of Ṭuwā: Its explanation has already passed in Surat Ṭā Hā. ** اذْهَبْ إِلى فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّهُ طَغى ** (17) ‘Go to Pharaoh, for he has rebelled.’ [This is understood] upon the intention of the saying (القول). ** فَقُلْ هَلْ لَكَ إِلى أَنْ تَزَكَّى ** (18) And say, ‘Will you purify yourself?’ Do you have an inclination to purify yourself from disbelief (الكفر) and transgression (الطغيان)? And it was recited (قُرِئَ) تزكّى with the doubling (تشديد) of the zāy (الزاي). ** وَأَهْدِيَكَ إِلى رَبِّكَ فَتَخْشى ** (19) And I will guide you to your Lord, so that you may fear [Him].’ By performing the obligations (الواجبات) and abandoning the prohibitions (المحرّمات), since fear (الخشية) only occurs after knowledge (المعرفة). And this is like an explanation for His saying: فَقُولا لَهُ قَوْلاً لَيِّناً. (20) And speak to him with a gentle word. ** فَأَراهُ الْآيَةَ الْكُبْرى ** (20) Then he showed him the great sign. Meaning, he went and conveyed [the message], and then showed him the great miracle (المعجزة الكبرى). ** فَكَذَّبَ وَعَصى. (21) But he denied and disobeyed. ** ثُمَّ أَدْبَرَ يَسْعى ** (22) Then he turned his back, striving hard. He turned away from obedience, striving to invalidate His command. ** فَحَشَرَ ** (23) Then he mustered [his men], So he gathered his soldiers, فَنادى. (23) and proclaimed. ** فَقالَ أَنَا رَبُّكُمُ الْأَعْلى. (24) and said, ‘I am your Lord, the most high.’ ** فَأَخَذَهُ اللهُ نَكالَ الْآخِرَةِ وَالْأُولى ** (25) So Allah seized him with the punishment of the Hereafter and the former [life]. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) [said]: The punishment (النكال) is the penalty (العقوبة). The latter (الآخرة) is his saying: أَنَا رَبُّكُمُ الْأَعْلى (I am your Lord, the most high). And the former (الأُولى) is his saying: مَا عَلِمْتُ لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلَهٍ غَيْرِي (I do not know of any god for you other than me). So Allah destroyed him because of these two sayings. And in al-Khisal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, there were forty years between the two statements. And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: Gabriel said: "I said, 'O Lord, do You leave Pharaoh alone when he has said: فَقَالَ أَنَا رَبُّكُمُ الْأَعْلَى (and said, ‘I am your Lord, the most high’)?'" He (Allah) said: "Only one like you, who fears escape (الفوت), says this."

(٢٦) إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَعِبْرَةً لِمَنْ يَخْشى ** (26) There is indeed a warning in that for him who fears [Allah]. For him whose nature (شأنه) is fear (الخشية). (٢٧) أَأَنْتُمْ أَشَدُّ خَلْقاً أَمِ السَّماءُ بَناها. (27) Are you harder to create or the heaven that He built? (٢٨) رَفَعَ سَمْكَها فَسَوَّاها. (28) He raised its ceiling and proportioned it. (٢٩) وَأَغْطَشَ لَيْلَها He darkened it وَأَخْرَجَ ضُحاها and He brought forth the light of its sun. (29) He darkened its night and brought forth its forenoon. (٣٠) وَالْأَرْضَ بَعْدَ ذلِكَ دَحاها He spread it out and prepared it for habitation (للسّكنى). (30) And after that He spread out the earth. (٣١) أَخْرَجَ مِنْها ماءَها By causing the springs (العيون) to gush forth وَمَرْعاها. (31) He brought forth from it its water and its pasture. (٣٢) وَالْجِبالَ أَرْساها He fixed them firmly. (32) And the mountains He set firm. (٣٣) مَتاعاً لَكُمْ وَلِأَنْعامِكُمْ. (33) A provision for you and for your livestock. (٣٤) فَإِذا جاءَتِ الطَّامَّةُ The calamity (الداهية) which overwhelms (تطمّ), meaning, it rises above all other calamities (الدّواهي), الْكُبْرى (the Great) which is the greatest of calamities (الطامات). (34) So when the Great Catastrophe comes— In al-Ikmal, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a tradition (حديث): Indeed, الطَّامَّةُ الْكُبْرى ** (the Great Catastrophe) is the emergence of the Beast of the Earth (دابّة الأرض). And the apodosis (جواب) of فَإِذا ** (So when) is omitted (محذوف), indicated by what follows it.

(٣٥) يَوْمَ يَتَذَكَّرُ الْإِنْسانُ ما سَعى ** (35) the day man will remember what he strove for, by seeing it recorded (مُدَوَّناً) in his scroll (صَحِيفَتِهِ), which he had forgotten due to excessive heedlessness (الغَفْلَة) and the long duration. القمّيّ said: He will remember all that he did. (٣٦) وَبُرِّزَتِ الْجَحِيمُ ** (36) and the Hellfire is brought into view He said: He said: And it is brought forth لِمَنْ يَرى ** (for whoever sees) for every beholder, such that it is not hidden from anyone. (٣٧) فَأَمَّا مَنْ طَغى. (37) As for him who was rebellious, In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a tradition: مَنْ طَغى ** (him who was rebellious) means he went astray intentionally (عَمْد) without proof (حُجَّة). (٣٨) وَآثَرَ الْحَياةَ الدُّنْيا ** (38) and preferred the life of the world, then he plunged into it and did not prepare for the Hereafter through worship (العِبَادَة) and the refinement of the soul (تَهْذِيب النَّفْس). (٣٩) فَإِنَّ الْجَحِيمَ هِيَ الْمَأْوى ** (39) the Hellfire will indeed be his abode. it is his abode. (٤٠) وَأَمَّا مَنْ خافَ مَقامَ رَبِّهِ ** (40) But as for him who feared the standing before his Lord, his standing before his Lord, due to his knowledge of the Origin (المَبْدَأ) and the Return (المَعَاد). وَنَهَى النَّفْسَ عَنِ الْهَوى ** (41) and restrained his soul from the caprice, due to his knowledge that caprice (الهَوَى) destroys him. (٤١) فَإِنَّ الْجَنَّةَ هِيَ الْمَأْوى ** (42) the Garden will indeed be his abode. القمّيّ said: He is the servant who, when he encounters disobedience to Allah and has the power to commit it, then abandons it out of fear of Allah and restrains the soul from it, his reward (مُكَافَأَتُهُ) is the Garden (الجَنَّة). And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Whoever knows that Allah sees him and hears what He says and does, and knows what he performs of good or evil, and this knowledge restrains him from ugly deeds (القَبِيح مِنَ الأَعْمَال), that is the one who خافَ مَقامَ رَبِّهِ وَنَهَى النَّفْسَ عَنِ الْهَوى** (feared the standing before his Lord and restrained his soul from the caprice)."

(٤٢) يَسْئَلُونَكَ عَنِ السَّاعَةِ أَيَّانَ مُرْساها ** (42) They ask you about the Hour, when will be its advent? When is its anchoring (إرساؤها)? Meaning, its establishment (إقامتها) and confirmation (إثباتها). Al-Qummi said: When will it stand?

(٤٣)** فِيمَ أَنْتَ مِنْ ذِكْراها ** (43) What concern have you with mentioning it? In what thing are you concerning the mention of its time to them? Meaning, you are not concerned with mentioning it to them and clarifying its time in any way, for indeed, that is among the matters which Allah has reserved exclusively for His knowledge.

(٤٤) إِلى رَبِّكَ مُنْتَهاها ** (44) Its term is up to your Lord. Meaning, the limit (منتهى) of its knowledge. Al-Qummi said: Meaning, its knowledge is with Allah.

(٤٥) إِنَّما أَنْتَ مُنْذِرُ مَنْ يَخْشاها. (45) You are only a warner for him who fears it.

(٤٦) كَأَنَّهُمْ يَوْمَ يَرَوْنَها لَمْ يَلْبَثُوا ** (46) The day they see it, it will be as if they had not remained [in the world] Meaning, in the world (في الدنيا). ** إِلَّا عَشِيَّةً أَوْ ضُحاها ** except for an evening or its forenoon. Meaning, the evening (عشيّة) of a day or its forenoon (ضحاها), like His saying: ** إِلَّا ساعَةً مِنَ النَّهارِ ** (except an hour of the day). And for this reason, He attributed the forenoon (الضحى) to the evening (العشيّة), because they are in one day. Al-Qummi said: Part of a day.

في ثواب الأعمال والمجمع عن الصادق عليه السلام : In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites Wa al-Nāzi‘āt (والنّازعات) will not die except while being quenched (ريّاناً), and Allah will not resurrect him except while being quenched (ريّاناً), and he will not enter Paradise except while being quenched (ريّاناً).
